title: The First Aircraft | V1 Built in San Francisco
detail:
V1 was Skyways' first aircraft, a Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) fixed-wing quadplane with 5 electric motors built on a Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) foam wing modified for VTOL propulsion. Despite its simplicity, V1 flew a fully autonomous multi-point cargo mission: takeoff from point A, fly to point B to pick up a small box, deliver it to point C, and return to A, all without human intervention. An onboard Companion Computer ran an early version of Skyways' precision landing code to execute the mission end-to-end. Endurance was under 30 minutes with a 2-lb cargo capacity.

Charles' father, also passionate about aviation, flew to San Francisco multiple times in 2016 to help build V1, serving as an unofficial co-founder during this earliest phase. For flight testing, Charles would drive almost every day to the Pacifica coast near San Francisco, and later to Livermore where there was more open space to fly.

title: Skyways Air Transportation Founded
detail: Charles Acknin, born near Airbus HQ in southern France, co-founded Skyways in Austin, TX with Chris Craighill. Acknin left Google after nearly 7 years as a senior software engineer on core search. The company began 'not with a fuselage but with code,' reflecting Acknin's software-first philosophy applied to autonomous flight. Delaware incorporation filed February 13, 2017.
charles_corrections: Round II: Only two co-founders, Anurag Sabbarwal was not a co-founder

title: Used Firefly's Office & Rocket Test Site, Cedar Park, TX
detail: Charles Acknin along with co-founder Chris Craighill (formerly at Firefly Aerospace) secured access to Firefly's shop and office in Cedar Park and their rocket test site at Briggs, TX for flight testing.

title: Start of Early Aircraft Prototyping | All-Electric Phase
detail: Skyways began iterating through initial all-electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) configurations, building small-scale aircraft for proof-of-concept testing and autonomous flight validation.
charles_corrections: Source attribution updated: this entry exists because Charles flagged the pre-founding all-electric prototyping phase as a missing timeline milestone.

title: Manor Office | 5,000 SF Expansion
detail: First official Skyways office and shop in Manor, TX at 5,000 square feet. Replaced the temporary Firefly shared space with a dedicated facility for aircraft development.

title: Y Combinator S17 Batch
detail: Accepted into Y Combinator's Summer 2017 batch. Raised $120K pre-seed from Y Combinator and began building the initial aircraft prototypes in the Bay Area before relocating to Austin, TX.

title: V2.0 Built & Flown | First Carbon Fiber Composite Airframe
detail: Skyways built and flew V2.0, a small quadplane X8 with a 12ft wingspan, the company's first carbon fiber composite airframe. V2.0 was demonstrated to investors for fundraising purposes as part of the seed round. The aircraft is still displayed on the ceiling of the Tech Ridge shop.
charles_corrections: Round III: V2.0 built and flown in 2017, small quadplane X8, 12ft wingspan, first composite airframe

title: Start of Seed Fundraising | Rolling Close
detail: Skyways began raising its seed round on a rolling basis, bringing in early capital to fund aircraft development and operations. $120K pre-seed from Y Combinator, followed by a $1M seed round led by Capital Factory with 12 additional investors.

title: V2.1 & V2.2 | First 18ft Composite Airframes
detail: In the second half of 2017, Skyways started building V2.1 and V2.2, a larger 18ft wingspan, 140-lb All-Up Weight (AUW) composite airframe. The team crashed a lot and learned a lot during this intensive build and test phase.
charles_corrections: Round III: Second half of 2017, started V2.1 and V2.2, 18ft wingspan, 140-lb AUW, crashed a lot

title: $272,711 | USMC/GTRI First Purchase Order (Two V2.2 Aircraft)
detail: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), in partnership with the Georgia Institute of Technology / Georgia Tech Research Institute (GTRI), issued Skyways' very first purchase order for two V2.2 aircraft under federal prime contract FA8075-14-D-0018-0018 (USAF AFLCMC). Combined value $272,711.48 across two purchase orders. This was Skyways' first-ever contract and a critical early validation of the platform's readiness for military customers.
sources: Skyways Internal | GTRI POs #0000441708 ($213,967.48, Nov 2, 2017) + #0000498568 ($58,744.00, Jun 1, 2018) under FA8075-14-D-0018-0018, not publicly disclosed
charles_corrections: Round III: This happened end of 2017 — first ever contract/PO. Navy Blue Water program only happened later in 2019.
Date corrected from 2017-12 to 2017-11 per signed PO #0000441708 dated Nov 2, 2017
bullets: PO #0000441708, Nov 2, 2017: two V2.2 UAS, integration and flight tests, USMC training, test support, crating and shipping
PO #0000498568, Jun 1, 2018: CDR Preparation, CDR Execution, two sets aircraft containers and auxiliary equipment containers

title: USMC V2.2 Delivery & White Sands BVLOS Demo
detail: Skyways finished building the first two V2.2 aircraft for the United States Marine Corps (USMC) and drove to New Mexico for flight testing and demonstration at White Sands with New Mexico State University (NMSU), Georgia Tech Research Institute (GTRI), and USMC. This marked the first time Skyways ever flew Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) and completed a greater than 60-mile flight, still all-electric at the time.
charles_corrections: Round III: Apr 2018, first two V2.2 for USMC, White Sands NM demo with NMSU + GTRI + USMC, first BVLOS, >60-mile flight, still all electric

title: ANTX 2019 | Skyways Beats Boeing in Navy Competition
detail: Skyways won the Advanced Naval Technology Exercise (ANTX) 2019 competition organized by the U.S. Navy to demonstrate autonomous cargo resupply to a naval vessel on the Patuxent River, MD. The 18.5-ft wingspan drone carried a 20-lb payload 40 miles to a moving vessel, landed, and returned autonomously. Skyways was selected out of 65 companies, beating Boeing's offering.

title: $575,141 | Navy OTA Award (Blue Water Maritime Logistics UAS)
detail: Following the ANTX 2019 competition win, the U.S. Navy awarded an Other Transaction Authority (OTA) contract to Skyways ($575,141 base) for the Blue Water Maritime Logistics Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) program. Contract number N00421-19-9-0007, awarded August 29, 2019 by NAWCAD. This first Navy contract specifically funded the development of hybrid propulsion for the V2 platform. Through subsequent modifications, the funded value grew to $2,066,701.73 by July 2022 (Total Potential Amount including CMG Fee: $2,476,005.91).
charles_corrections: Round III: The very first US Navy contract (2019) was specifically to do the hybrid propulsion.

title: V2 Platform Development Accelerates
detail: Original Navy requirements drove V2 specs: 20-lb payload (one cubic foot cargo volume) or approximately 400 nautical mile range. Payload and range represent a tradeoff: maximum payload reduces range and vice versa. Hybrid fixed-wing design with 8 electric motors for Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) plus internal combustion engine for cruise flight. The platform iterates through sub-variants (V2.2, V2.5, V2.6, V2.6b) over subsequent years.

title: End of Seed Fundraising | Rolling Close
detail: Skyways raised its seed round on a rolling basis over the first several years of the company, bringing in capital as needed rather than in a single close. Early seed investors included Capital Factory, Social Capital, NextView Ventures, and others.
charles_corrections: Round III: Removed UpHonest. Outlander VC and MVP Ventures came in later (2021+ Series A), not seed.

title: End of Early Aircraft Prototyping | All-Electric Phase
detail: Skyways iterated through initial all-electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) configurations. Aircraft were small-scale, used for proof-of-concept flights, and evolved rapidly through multiple designs before the Navy contract drove the shift to hybrid propulsion.
sources: Commercial UAV News | Pursuit of Autonomous Deliveries (May 2020)

title: $575,141 | Navy OTA Spiral Development Begins
detail: Following the initial Navy Other Transaction Authority (OTA) contract award, spiral development began with follow-on awards expanding the scope of Skyways' hybrid propulsion logistics Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) work. The Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWCAD) acquired a Skyways prototype to demonstrate long-range ship-to-ship and ship-to-shore cargo transport at Naval Air Station (NAS) Patuxent River.

title: V2 Flight Test Campaign | V2.2 to V2.6b Sub-Variants
detail: Continuous refinement through sub-variants (V2.2, V2.5, V2.6, V2.6b). Flight testing near Giddings, TX with progressive capability additions driven by Navy operational requirements.
charles_corrections: Round III: Removed SkyNav reference (SkyNav started Q2 2023, not 2020). Removed Starlink reference (Starlink started end of 2024, not 2020).

title: V3 Initial Concept
detail: Skyways begins initial concept work on the V3 next-generation platform. Current performance estimates target 157 nautical miles range with 100 lbs payload, or approximately 35 lbs cargo at 1,000 nautical miles, representing a significant leap in capability over the V2.
charles_corrections: Round III: V3 timeline provided — initial concept started 2021.

title: Carrier Deck Trials | USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78)
detail: Two weeks of autonomous Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) operations on the Navy's newest and most advanced aircraft carrier. V2.5 drone tested on the flight deck of USS Gerald R. Ford.
bullets: Selected from 65 candidate UAS evaluated by Navy (Aug 2019)
24-lb cargo / 65-mile range covers ~80% of critical spare parts needs
charles_corrections: Round IV: 500 mi range and 30-lb V2.5 payload claims are per Charles internal — not directly confirmed in publicly verified sources (Maritime Executive verified the 65-UAS / 24 lbs / 65 mi / 80% figures).

title: $47,775 | USAF SBIR Phase I
detail: Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Phase I under topic AF203-CSO1, contract number FA8649-21-P-0539, project title 'Autonomous transportation using AI and a long-range vertical-lift UAS'. Award period February 4, 2021 to May 7, 2021. This $47,775 Phase I award began the Air Force Works (AFWERX) SBIR pathway that would eventually lead to the USAF awarding Skyways a $37M contract.
charles_corrections: Round III: Rephrased from 'lead to $37M' to 'eventually lead to the USAF awarding Skyways a $37M contract'.

title: Cross Park Drive | 10,000 SF Austin Facility
detail: Facility expansion to 10,000 square feet on Cross Park Drive in Austin to support Navy contract execution and growing team.

title: Ship-to-Ship Autonomous Flights
detail: V2 flies between USS Bainbridge (DDG-96) and USNS Joshua Humphreys (T-AO-188). These flights were conducted entirely by U.S. Navy personnel with no Skyways employees on either ship, demonstrating the system's operational maturity and ease of use by military operators.
charles_corrections: Round III: Removed computer vision language — CV approach changed over time and the original description wasn't accurate for this period.

title: Orbital UAV Partnership | Heavy Fuel Engine MoU
detail: Skyways signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Orbital Corporation (ASX: OEC), a Perth, Australia-based propulsion company, for a pre-production heavy fuel engine to be integrated into Skyways' next-generation Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) for the U.S. Navy cargo transportation program. The engine was scheduled for delivery in Q1 2022. Orbital CEO Todd Alder called it 'an exciting new development within the unmanned market,' while Skyways CEO Charles Acknin noted the company was 'continuing to develop our offering to ensure we meet the rigorous demands of ship-to-ship and shore-to-ship logistics.'

title: Series A | $13M (Thursday Ventures Lead)
detail: Skyways raised a $13M Series A led by Thursday Ventures, joined by MVP Ventures, Outlander VC, Defense Angels, Cathexis, Capital Factory, and other follow-on investors. Total raised at the close of the Series A was $17M.
charles_corrections: Round III: Series A was missing entirely from the timeline. Added per Charles.
Note: Crunchbase lists $15M; Charles confirmed $13M. Charles figure is authoritative.

title: $1,807,149 | DIU/USN OTA (Blue Water + Spiral Mods)
detail: Skyways secured a contract through the Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) for the Blue Water Maritime Logistics UAS program in partnership with the U.S. Navy, Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWCAD), and Military Sealift Command. The award contract was directly issued by Army Contracting Command, New Jersey (ACC-NJ). DIU partnered with the 4th Fleet and Marine Corps Warfighting Laboratory to develop computer vision technology enabling autonomous navigation and intelligent landing without beacon assistance.
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract W15QKN-22-9-5005 (base $1,429,587.50 Feb 3, 2022; P00004 mod brought funded total to $1,807,149.22 as of Mar 20, 2024; not publicly disclosed)
Inside Unmanned Systems | Navy Demonstrating Programs for Drone Delivery at Sea
Potomac Officers Club | US Naval Components Test Skyways Blue Water Logistics UAS
charles_corrections: Round III: Corrected from ~2023 to 2022. Charles confirmed: 'We won the DIU contract/OTA in 2022.'
Date corrected from 2022-01 to 2022-02 per signed contract W15QKN-22-9-5005 effective Feb 3, 2022 (signed by Charles Acknin and Norman R. Bonano, Army Contracting Command NJ)

Round IV: DIU Blue Water program target (50 lbs over 250 mi for ~95% of critical parts) is per Charles internal — public sources confirm contract structure but not the specific program target percentage.
bullets: Program target (per Charles): 50 lbs over 250 mi for ship-based logistics, covers ~95% of critical parts by weight

title: $749,711 | USAF SBIR Phase II
detail: Air Force Works (AFWERX) Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Phase II under topic AF203-CSO1, contract number FA8649-22-P-0719, project title '12-Hour Continuous Inter-Unit Autonomous UAS Resupply'. Award period March 10, 2022 to June 12, 2023. Funds further development along the pathway that would eventually lead to the $37M Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract.

title: V3 Subscale | First Test Flights
detail: Skyways builds and flies a subscale version of the V3 platform, culminating in test flights in October 2022. This validates the aerodynamic design and hybrid propulsion concept before committing to full-scale development.
charles_corrections: Round III: V3 subscale with test flights in Oct 2022.

title: $2,066,702 | Navy OTA Final Value (Blue Water Spiral Complete)
detail: After the initial hybrid propulsion contract, the Navy Other Transaction Authority (OTA) expanded through multiple spiral development follow-on awards, growing to approximately four times its original value ($2.07M). These follow-on awards funded progressive capability demonstrations including folding wings development and increasingly complex ship-based operations.
charles_corrections: Round III: Clarified that spiral dev came after the initial hybrid propulsion work.

title: 200+ NM Cargo Delivery Demonstrations | NAS Patuxent River
detail: Multi-mode cargo delivery operations at Naval Air Station (NAS) Patuxent River. Skyways and Martin UAV demonstrated Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) cargo delivery capabilities.
sources: Skyways Internal | Charles Acknin (NAWCAD finding: 90% of critical mission failures repairable with under 20 lb payloads | public sources cite a separate 50 lb threshold for general logistics)
bullets: Per NAWCAD (Charles internal): 90% of critical mission failures repairable with under-20-lb payloads

title: V3 Block 0 | Full-Scale First Flight
detail: The first full-scale V3 aircraft (Block 0) achieves its maiden flight. This is a major engineering milestone transitioning from subscale proof-of-concept to a flyable full-size platform.
charles_corrections: Round III: V3 Block 0 (full scale) first flew early 2023.

title: $71,070 | Navy DLA PO | DGPS (SPE8EJ21D0022 PO 0408)
detail: Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) Purchase Order PFR006115, Order Number PO 0408, under prime contract SPE8EJ21D0022 (Noble Supply and Logistics is prime; Skyways is component supplier). PO dated March 15, 2023. Total funded value $71,070.00. Delivery to James Tomasic at Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division Rapid Prototyping Experimentation and Demonstration (NAWCAD RPED), Patuxent River, MD.
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract on File (Noble Purchase Order PFR006115 under DLA prime SPE8EJ21D0022; PO dated Mar 15, 2023; $71,070.00 total funded to Skyways)
bullets: Differential Global Positioning System (DGPS) hardware, 6 units at $1,897.50 each: $11,385.00
Engineering services (integration, ground testing, flight testing, quality assurance): $48,185.00

title: SkyNav Launch | Autonomous Multi-Aircraft Coordination
detail: Skyways launches SkyNav, its proprietary autonomous software platform that coordinates multi-aircraft operations and integrates with third-party Unmanned Traffic Management (UTM) systems. SkyNav becomes the ground control system used across all Skyways operations.
charles_corrections: Round III: SkyNav started Q2 2023, not 2020 as previously stated.

title: $479,053 | Navy NAWCAD BWUAS 2.0 Prototype
detail: U.S. Navy Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWCAD) awarded Skyways the Blue Water Maritime Logistics Unmanned Aircraft System 2.0 (BWUAS 2.0) Prototype Project contract through the Naval Aviation Systems Consortium (NASC), effective May 4, 2023.
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract N00421-23-9-0013 ($479,052.96, effective May 4, 2023, not publicly disclosed)
bullets: Lead division: NAWCAD Rapid Prototyping and Experimentation Division (RPED)
Focus: autonomous logistical replenishment (shore-to-ship, ship-to-ship, ship-to-shore)
Mods added: Project ULTRA Demonstration (May 2025), V3 TCP Development (May 2025)

title: $203,735 | Navy DLA PO | SATCOMv2 (SPE8EJ21D0022 PO 0463)
detail: Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) Purchase Order PFR007234, Order Number PO 0463, under prime contract SPE8EJ21D0022 (Noble Supply and Logistics is prime; Skyways is component supplier). PO dated May 26, 2023. Total funded value $203,735.00. Delivery to James Tomasic at NAWCAD RPED, Patuxent River, MD. Buyer: Tyler Ray (Noble).
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract on File (Noble Purchase Order PFR007234 under DLA prime SPE8EJ21D0022; PO dated May 26, 2023; $203,735.00 total funded to Skyways)
bullets: SKYTRAC DLS-100 SATCOMv2 hardware, 9 units across 3 aircraft at $28,865.00 each: $86,595.00
V2.6b SATCOMv2 Non-Recurring Engineering and Test (software, mechanical, electrical, test, flight test): $105,110.00
SKYTRAC DLS-100 Integration across 3 aircraft at $1,610.00 each: $4,830.00
SPP-720M airtime plan (720 MB per month for 12 months) at $600.00 each: $7,200.00

title: Fleet Battle Problem 23-1 | First Logistics Drone in a Navy Fleet Exercise
detail: Autonomous flights from USNS Patuxent to Marines at Camp Lejeune, NC during Fleet Battle Problem 23-1. Skyways team worked with Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division (NAWCAD), UX-24 Unmanned Test Squadron, United States Marine Corps (USMC), and Military Sealift Command (MSC). Five test flights with two drones demonstrating autonomous cargo delivery ship-to-shore and shore-to-ship.

title: $79,545 | Navy DLA PO | Intelligen (SPE8EJ21D0022 PO 0465)
detail: Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) Purchase Order PFR007445, Order Number PO 0465, under prime contract SPE8EJ21D0022 (Noble Supply and Logistics is prime; Skyways is component supplier). PO dated June 15, 2023. Total funded value $79,545.00 breaks out as: V2.6b Intelligen onboard start across 3 aircraft at $12,255.00 each = $36,765.00; V2.6b AV1-3 Intelligen integration and testing across 3 aircraft at $14,260.00 each = $42,780.00. Delivery to James Tomasic at NAWCAD RPED, Patuxent River, MD. Buyer: Karli King (Noble).
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract on File (Noble Purchase Order PFR007445 under DLA prime SPE8EJ21D0022; PO dated Jun 15, 2023; $79,545.00 total funded to Skyways)

title: V3 Block 1 | Full-Scale First Flight
detail: V3 Block 1 (full scale) achieves first flight. Significant resources dedicated to V3 from mid-2023 onward. Block 1 incorporates lessons from Block 0 and prepares the platform for customer demonstrations.
charles_corrections: Round III: V3 Block 1 (full scale) first flew early 2024.

title: Tech Ridge | 25,000 SF North Austin HQ
detail: New 25,000 square foot facility at Tech Ridge in North Austin provides manufacturing space for V3 production ramp. 750-acre test site near Giddings, TX used for flight testing.

title: RIMPAC 2024 | USS Curtis Wilbur (DDG-54)
detail: Three Skyways V2.6 aircraft launched and recovered from USS Curtis Wilbur (DDG-54) during Trident Warrior at the Rim of the Pacific Exercise (RIMPAC) 2024, the world's largest international maritime exercise. Simulated Just-In-Time Delivery of medical supplies and critical parts.
charles_corrections: Round III: Corrected from 6 UAS to 3 Skyways aircraft on that DDG.

Round III V.3 (Apr 1, 2026): Removed PteroDynamics X-P4 mention and 'six UAS' count framing per Charles ruling ('completely irrelevant with an all electric drone that can only fly 60 miles'). Leads now with three Skyways V2.6 aircraft.

title: $2,200,000 | USAF SBIR Phase 3 IDIQ + STRATFI Selection ($4,999,999 Ceiling)
detail: After submitting the Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) package, USAF AFWERX awarded Skyways the basic SBIR Phase 3 IDIQ contract FA228024D0003 in June 2024. The first Task Order FA228024F0013 was awarded simultaneously. STRATFI selection was announced in the same month and ultimately led to the $37M contract in May 2025.
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract FA228024D0003 / Task Order FA228024F0013 (~$2.2M obligated as of Jul 2024 across Mods #1 and #2; $4.99M IDIQ ceiling; not publicly disclosed)
charles_corrections: Round III: Cleaned up 'initial millions' language.
bullets: Early 2024: USAF AFWERX issued RFP for V3 development
Jun 19, 2024: IDIQ contract FA228024D0003 awarded; 5-year ordering period; $4,999,999 ceiling
Mods #1 + #2: Task Order FA228024F0013 obligations grew to ~$2.2M
Jan 6, 2025: Mod #3 extended CLIN dates (no $ change)
June 2024: STRATFI selection announced leading to the $37M contract awarded May 2025

title: DIU Temperature-Controlled Payload Demo with III MEF
detail: Under a Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) contract modification, Skyways demonstrated Temperature Controlled Payload (TCP) delivery capability for III Marine Expeditionary Force (III MEF), a U.S. Marine Corps command covering Indo-Pacific operations. The demonstration, conducted stateside, flew ten bags of blood at 3 to 6 degrees Celsius across more than 300 miles in a single six-hour flight.
charles_corrections: Round III: Reframed from 'III MEF operations' to accurately reflect this was a DIU contract mod for TCP demo. Not direct III MEF ops.

Round II V.2 (Mar 20, 2026): TCP demo happened stateside, not in Okinawa. III MEF is based in Okinawa but this specific demonstration did not. Added authoritative spec per Charles: six-hour flight, 300+ miles, ten bags of blood at 3-6 C. Ref deck: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1dE6P1A2Vf7oIlt4TNG11nQ8Mo5Q8FX0AcSQQtI-L-Go

title: JMSDF | First Autonomous Resupply to Destroyer Akizuki
detail: Skyways conducts Japan's first autonomous Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) drone cargo resupply to a naval vessel at sea. From August 15 to 25, 2024, the Skyways V2.6, operated by ANA Holdings, delivered cargo including food supplies to the JMSDF destroyer DD-115 Akizuki, operating 23 kilometers offshore Minami-Boso, Chiba Prefecture. The V2.6 completed the fully autonomous round trip (launch, transit, deck landing on a moving ship, and return) in approximately 40 minutes with no human intervention. All flights conducted Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS). This marks Skyways' first operational engagement with an allied nation's navy.
sources: drone.jp | JMSDF Japan's First VTOL Drone Supply to Vessel
charles_corrections: Round III: JSDF operations were entirely missing from the timeline. Added per Charles.
Date corrected from 2024-07 to 2024-08 per JMSDF official post confirming Aug 15-25, 2024
Ship identified as DD-115 Akizuki (Akizuki-class destroyer) per Japanese-language sources

title: JASDF Inter-Island Resupply
detail: Skyways conducts inter-island autonomous cargo resupply with the Japan Air Self-Defense Force (JASDF) through ANA Holdings. All flights conducted Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS). Demonstrates Skyways' capability for allied nation military logistics across maritime and island environments.
charles_corrections: Round III: JSDF operations were entirely missing from the timeline. Added per Charles.

AUDIT NOTE (Apr 14, 2026): Exhaustive search in English and Japanese found zero public sources. JASDF has no known cargo drone program. October 2024 date matches the JMSDF announcement date (Oct 28) for the separate Aug 2024 destroyer resupply (ID 29). Needs verification with Charles — may be a separate undisclosed operation or possible conflation with ID 29.

title: CVSI | First End-to-End Autonomous Ship Intercept
detail: Under the Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) Other Transaction Authority (OTA) contract, Skyways completed the first end-to-end Computer Vision Ship Intercept (CVSI) demonstration, flying a V2.6 aircraft from shore to a U.S. Navy vessel in the Gulf of Mexico with no GPS beacon on the target ship. Onboard computer vision visually acquired the vessel from the horizon, flew a triangular approach pattern along an intercept corridor, then handed off to Skyways' precision landing software for final approach. This was the final contractual milestone (Milestone 6) of the DIU CVSI sub-effort, which had been built in phases since 2022 alongside the parent RIMPAC 2024 program. No other company has demonstrated end-to-end computer-vision ship intercept at this scale.
charles_corrections: Round II V.2 #7 (Mar 20, 2026): Added per Charles — 'In Nov 2024 we FINALLY did CVSI, full Computer Vision Ship Intercept, demo to USN on a ship in the Gulf of Mexico. No other company has ever done this.' Date and location per Charles (trumps the June 2024 RIMPAC framing in the Inside Unmanned Systems article, which covers the earlier phase of the same DIU CVSI program).

title: Starlink Connectivity Added
detail: Skyways adds Starlink satellite connectivity to its aircraft, transforming real-time operations. CEO Acknin described the improvement: 'Before Starlink, it felt like the AOL days... Now it is like watching Netflix.'
sources: The Drone Girl | From cargo to commuters (Dec 2025)
charles_corrections: Round III: Starlink started end of 2024, not 2020 as previously listed.

title: First International Aircraft Deliveries
detail: Skyways delivers aircraft to commercial customers internationally for the first time. Skyports Drone Services takes delivery around February 2025, followed by ANA Holdings in approximately April 2025.
charles_corrections: Round III: Removed last sentence about this being the first time customers operated their own aircraft — the US Navy was flying their own Skyways aircraft for years before 2025.

title: Aircraft Lease Agreement | Navy NSLP-CRADA-NAWCADPAX-25-533 (V2 AV1-3)
detail: Non-Standard Limited Purpose Cooperative Research and Development Agreement (NSLP-CRADA) between Naval Air Warfare Center Aircraft Division Patuxent River (NAVAIRWARCENACDIV) and Skyways Air Transportation, Inc. Agreement number NSLP-CRADA-NAWCADPAX-25-533, titled "SKYWAYS V2.6 UNMANNED AERIAL SYSTEM DEMONSTRATION." Signed by Charles Acknin (Skyways CEO) on March 18, 2025 and by J.E. Dougherty IV, RDML, USN, Commander NAWCAD on March 20, 2025. Duration up to 4 years from effective date (through ~March 2029). No funds are transferred under the agreement (Article 16); Skyways retains title to its own equipment and must return all Navy-owned equipment at termination. Per Article 19, the agreement is approved for public use. No press release has been issued. Navy transfers custody of the following equipment to Skyways:
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract on File (NSLP-CRADA, approved for public use)

title: $37M | U.S. Air Force STRATFI Contract
detail: Skyways awarded a $37M ceiling contract through Air Force Works (AFWERX) Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI), one of the largest STRATFI awards ever given to an autonomous drone company. SBIR Phase II contract number FA2280-25-9-9523, awarded May 6, 2025 with period of performance through May 6, 2029, project title 'SKYWAYS V3 300LB Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) Unmanned Aerial System (UAS) Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI)'. The SBIR Phase II portion is $3,550,613, with the full $37M ceiling reached through STRATFI's 1:1:2 Government:SBIR:Private matching structure. Funds V3 transition from prototype to full-rate production. STRATFI is a pathway to a Program of Record (PoR), a formal Department of Defense (DoD) acquisition designation that unlocks large-scale, long-term procurement.
charles_corrections: Round III: Added Program of Record context. STRATFI is a pathway to PoR, which is a big deal for Skyways.

title: $361,832 | Navy CLIN 0005 (Project ULTRA Demonstration)
detail: U.S. Navy NAWCAD added Contract Line Item 0005 (CLIN 0005) to Skyways' BWUAS 2.0 contract via Modification P00002 effective May 23, 2025, funding Skyways' Project ULTRA Demonstration.
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract N00421-23-9-0013 Mod P00002, CLIN 0005 ($361,832.40, signed May 23, 2025, not publicly disclosed)
bullets: Mission: shore-to-shore long-range autonomous cargo flights
Route: Grand Forks AFB to Cavalier SFS, North Dakota
Total task order: $3.5M (OUSD + Grand Forks County)

title: $982,850 | Navy CLIN 0006 (V3 Temperature-Controlled Payload)
detail: U.S. Navy NAWCAD added Contract Line Item 0006 (CLIN 0006) to Skyways' BWUAS 2.0 contract via Modification P00002 effective May 23, 2025. CLIN 0006 funds the design and build of an actively temperature-controlled payload proof of concept for the V3 Block 2 aircraft.
sources: Skyways Internal | Contract N00421-23-9-0013 Mod P00002, CLIN 0006 ($982,850.39, signed May 23, 2025, not publicly disclosed)
bullets: System: end-to-end, powered by onboard alternator
Deliverables: PDR within 6 months, CDR thereafter

title: $7M Private Capital Raise + $5M Debt Facility for STRATFI Match
detail: Skyways raised $7M in private equity capital to meet the Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) 1:1:2 Government:Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR):Private matching structure. Additionally, Skyways secured a $5M debt facility from defense-specialist lender Leonid Capital Partners to accelerate V3 production for the Department of Defense (DoD).
charles_corrections: Round III: Corrected — $7M equity was separate from $5M debt. Not $7M total including debt.

title: Project ULTRA | 20 Operational Flights for DoD
detail: Sole Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) provider for Project ULTRA (UAS Logistics, Traffic, Response and Autonomy), sponsored by the Office of the Under Secretary of Defense (OUSD).
bullets: 20 operational flights (10 round trips) over 12 days
Route: Grand Forks AFB to Cavalier Space Force Station, ND
Cargo: 230 lbs total (23 lbs per round trip), including temperature-controlled blood
Milestone (per Skyways): first unmanned, fully autonomous Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) cargo flights between two U.S. airports

title: V3 Block 1 | First Customer Flights
detail: V3 flies first customer missions during Project ULTRA at GrandSKY, ND. Nearly 200 test flights completed including 8-hour heavy-fuel engine endurance flights. First customer demonstration of V3 Block 1 flying Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) in the national airspace.

title: 11 Consecutive Resupply Flights in One Day
detail: V2 operational record demonstrating the operational tempo required for fleet-scale logistics.
sources: Skyways Internal | Charles Acknin (V2 operational record: 11 flights / 9.4 hrs / 466 NM / 8-min turnarounds, Sept 2025)

title: Commercial Contract | RWE Offshore Wind Farm (Baltic Sea, Germany)
detail: Germany's first long-range Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) offshore wind drone delivery. Partnered with RWE and Skyports Drone Services at the Arkona wind farm in the Baltic Sea. V2 flew 40km routes, up to 10kg cargo, sub-30-minute flights vs. 1+ hour by boat. Over 2,500km total, 65+ flight hours in 3 weeks.

title: ANA Holdings Partnership & $1M Investment
detail: ANA Holdings invested $1M through its corporate venture fund and signed a long-term business partnership agreement. Skyways drones fly between Okinawa islands delivering medical supplies. ANA established internal Skyways-specific pilot training.

title: First Public Demo Day | Media Debut
detail: Skyways hosted its first-ever press Demo Day near Austin. Journalist Sally French of The Drone Girl published the most comprehensive independent profile of the company. KXAN Austin (NBC) provided TV coverage. This marked Skyways' formal public introduction after years of focused execution.

title: Leadership Team Expansion
detail: Key hires strengthen commercial, regulatory, and flight operations leadership. Isaac Roberts transitions from Chief Commercial Officer to Chief Strategy Officer. Skyways finished 2025 at approximately 39 employees and has doubled revenue for three consecutive years.
charles_corrections: Round II CA#17 (Mar 20, 2026): Charles asked to verify end-of-2025 headcount (~39). Anthony (Skyways People Ops) confirmed "yes" via DM on Mar 19, 2026. Added ~39 employees claim to detail with Anthony as source.
bullets: Mike Uffelman, Head of Flight Ops, ex-U.S. Air Force, 2,500+ flight hours

title: ANA Completes Okinawa Medical Delivery Trials
detail: Long-distance pharmaceutical and blood sample deliveries across Okinawa Prefecture completed using Skyways Vertical Takeoff and Landing (VTOL) drones. Routes: Itoman City to Kumejima Town (approximately 100km in 1 hour 4 minutes) and Itoman City to Nago City (approximately 89km in 1 hour 1 minute). Cargo included prescription pharmaceuticals and research blood samples in temperature-controlled containers. Trials ran October 2025 through January 2026. Selected under Japan's Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism (MLIT) Drone Delivery Base Development Promotion Project.

title: Series B Round Opens
detail: Skyways begins its Series B fundraising round to accelerate V3 production ramp and expand commercial operations.

title: V3 Block 2 | First Article Build
detail: V3 Block 2 (production-intent) first article build scheduled for June 2026. This is the production design variant that will enter full-rate manufacturing.
charles_corrections: Round III: V3 Block 2 first article build scheduled Jun 2026.

title: V3 Production Ramp
detail: V3 Block 2 (production-intent) expected in service second half of 2026. Program of Record designation underway to unlock large-scale Department of Defense (DoD) procurement.

title: V3.2 | Forthcoming
detail: Next iteration in the V3 family beyond V3 Block 2. Details TBD; entry will be filled with full specs and milestone context once the build phase begins. This placeholder ensures the iteration list in the '20+ UAVs Built' stat menu is auditable against the timeline.
sources: Skyways Internal | Charles Acknin (V3.2 / V3 Block 2L planned for Q4 2026; placeholder pending more information)

title: V3 Full-Rate Production Target
detail: Full-rate V3 production targeted with Program of Record designation complete. Per CEO Acknin: 'We are really spending 2026 to build that production capability so that in 2027 we are ready to hit go.'
